content:
# RouteZA CMS Build

## Slide 1: CMS Build Overview
This section works as an appendix to the main RouteZA site case study. It focuses on the CMS build that supports launch readiness, content governance and SEO foundations.

## Suggested Image
- Strapi dashboard homepage or content manager overview.
- A clean screenshot of the CMS with RouteZA content types visible.

## SEO Callout
- The CMS was planned to make SEO fields editable without developer support.
- This supports faster updates to titles, descriptions, canonicals and structured content.

## Slide 2: Why A CMS Was Needed
The marketing site needed a simple editing workflow so new pages, articles and transport content could be added without hard-coding every update. The goal was to prepare RouteZA for launch in April with a structure that could scale.

## Suggested Image
- Before-and-after visual showing static content versus editable CMS content.
- Simple diagram of content flowing from Strapi into the website.

## SEO Callout
- A CMS helps keep on-page SEO consistent as content grows.
- It reduces the risk of missing metadata when new pages are published.

## Slide 3: Content Model And Structure
The CMS was set up with reusable page sections, blog content, news content, redirects and SEO fields. This gives the site a flexible structure while keeping content patterns tidy and repeatable.

## Suggested Image
- Screenshot of the page content type.
- Screenshot of reusable blocks or components inside Strapi.

## SEO Callout
- Reusable content blocks support consistent heading structure and cleaner page layouts.
- Separate content types make it easier to target search intent across evergreen pages, blog posts and news.

## Slide 4: SEO-Ready Fields In The CMS
Each page can store a meta title, meta description, canonical URL and noindex setting. That means SEO essentials are built into the publishing workflow from the start rather than added later.

## Suggested Image
- Screenshot of the SEO field group in the CMS.
- Close-up image of title, description and canonical inputs.

## SEO Callout
- This setup makes technical SEO checks part of content entry.
- It gives better control over indexation and reduces duplication risk.

## Slide 5: Editorial Workflow Before Launch
As of March, the focus is not on reporting live SEO results yet. The work is on launch preparation: structuring pages, reviewing metadata, setting up redirects and making sure future content can be published quickly.

## Suggested Image
- Simple timeline labelled March preparation and April launch.
- Workflow graphic showing draft, review and publish steps.

## SEO Callout
- This is pre-launch SEO work, so the value is in readiness rather than rankings.
- The strongest story here is building a site that can launch with sound SEO basics in place.

## Slide 6: Redirects And Content Hygiene
The CMS includes redirect management so route changes and legacy URLs can be handled cleanly. This helps protect crawl paths and supports a smoother experience for both users and search engines.

## Suggested Image
- Screenshot of the redirects collection in Strapi.
- Simple flow diagram showing an old URL pointing to a new URL.

## SEO Callout
- Redirect handling is important for preserving search equity.
- It also reduces the risk of broken journeys after launch updates.

## Slide 7: What This Enables After April Launch
Once the site is live, the CMS will support regular publishing, SEO page updates and a more structured internal content workflow. It also creates a clean base for measuring search performance properly after launch.

## Suggested Image
- Roadmap visual with launch first, then content growth and reporting.
- Light dashboard mock-up showing future KPIs such as impressions, clicks and indexed pages.

## SEO Callout
- Post-launch reporting can focus on index coverage, rankings, impressions, clicks and organic landing pages.
- For the interview, it is more credible to present these as planned measurement areas rather than live wins before launch.

## Slide 8: Key Takeaway
The CMS build is not just a backend task. It is part of the SEO foundation for RouteZA because it shapes how content is created, published and maintained from day one.

## Suggested Image
- Final summary slide with a clean CMS screenshot and short labels.
- Optional branded mock-up combining site front-end and CMS back-end.

## SEO Callout
- Good SEO starts with structure, not only content.
- The CMS makes that structure practical for launch and sustainable after launch.
metaTitle: RouteZA CMS Build Case Study | Pre-Launch SEO Setup
metaDescription: Appendix case study for the RouteZA CMS build, covering content structure, editable SEO fields, redirects and pre-launch preparation ahead of April launch.
